rationale: The page provides detailed information on four distinct scientific and technological topics: Altermagnets, Accelerometers, Desalination Technologies, and Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS). Each section includes definitions, principles, and applications, making it highly suitable for generating multiple-choice questions.
cleanedText:
Material: Made of strong substances (steel or titanium) and are insulated from extreme heat or cold.
8.9.6. ALTERMAGNETS
Scientists from S.N. Bose National Centre for Basic Sciences (SNBNCBS) discovered a unique duality in altermagnets using chromium antimonide (CrSb).
About Altermagnets
• Definition: Altermagnets are a new type of magnetic material that combine the best features of ferromagnets and antiferromagnets.
o Ferromagnets: Spins align in the same direction, resulting in strong magnetism.
o Antiferromagnets: Spins alternate, thus cancelling each other out, resulting in no external magnetism.
• Features:
o Like antiferromagnets, they exhibit alternating spins and therefore have no net magnetism.
o But due to their unique crystal symmetry, they show intense internal spin polarisation, similar to ferromagnets.
• CrSb (Chromium Antimonide) shows the strongest spin-splitting among known altermagnets.
8.9.7. ACCELEROMETER
Google's Android Earthquake Alert system has detected over 18,000 earthquakes using phone accelerometer signals.
About Accelerometer
• A sensing device that measures acceleration and detects the frequency and intensity of human movement.
• Principle of Functioning: Accelerometers work according to Newton’s law of motion, the force on an object is equal to its mass times its acceleration.
• Can measure both: Static acceleration (due to gravity) & dynamic acceleration (car crash).
• Key Applications
o Digital Devices: smartphones, Laptops, digital cameras- for rotating the display
o Vehicles: Used to trigger airbags
o Drones: to stabilize their orientation mid-flight
8.9.8. DESALINATION TECHNOLOGIES
IIT Bombay Scientists Developed a new hydrophobic Graphene-based lotus leaf-like solar evaporators that can facilitate water desalination.
Key Desalination Technologies
• Thermal Technology
o Heating of saline water and collecting the condensed vapor (distillate) to produce pure water.
o Usage: Mainly for seawater desalination
o E.g., Low Temperature Thermal Desalination (LTTD) plants, Lakshadweep
• Membrane Technology
o Feedwater is pumped through semi-permeable membranes to filter out the dissolved solids.
o Usage: Mainly for brackish water desalination
o E.g., Nemmeli Seawater Desalination Plant, Tamil Nadu, on Reverse Osmosis. (Largest desalination plant in the South Asia)
8.9.9. ADVANCED DRIVER ASSISTANCE SYSTEM
Union Government is considering mandating Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) in all new models of trucks and buses, including school buses.
